title: Kamma, Rebirth and Transcendence : A top down view of the Buddhadhamma..To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash pluginTwenty Cases Suggestive of Reincarnation - Ian Stevenson
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and coherent treatment of both kamma and rebirth within a single discourse, offering a sophisticated 'top-down' cosmological framework that encompasses transcendence, the Nibbana element, dependent origination, the six realms, and scientific evidence for rebirth (Ian Stevenson's research). It weaves together philosophical depth with accessible explanations, creation mythology, and practical implications, making it an ideal spine onto which complementary material can be grafted.
summary: This discourse presents a comprehensive view of kamma and rebirth from a transcendent perspective, explaining how the Buddha nature descends into the psychophysical organism and creates suffering through delusion. It covers the relationship between ethics, cosmology, the six realms of existence, and scientific research on rebirth, providing both philosophical depth and practical understanding.

relevance.note: Primary focus on kamma and rebirth from transcendent perspective
